title: Performance Benchmark
description: A measurable standard used to assess athlete readiness, track progress, and enforce goal profile prerequisites. Benchmarks can be time-based, load-based, rep-based, or skill-based.

name.description: Human-readable name. e.g. 'Back Squat (bodyweight ratio)', '3-Mile Run', 'Secret Service Snatch Test'

domain.description: Which modality this benchmark primarily measures.

description.description: What this benchmark tests and why it matters.

metric_type.description: The unit in which this benchmark is measured. Determines how level thresholds are compared.

lower_is_better.description: True for time-based benchmarks (faster = better). False for load, reps, distance benchmarks.

test_protocol.description: How to administer this test. Include warm-up requirements, equipment, and single vs. repeated effort.

levels.description: Performance thresholds by tier. At minimum define the tiers relevant to this benchmark. Values match metric_type.

goal_relevance.description: Goal profile IDs for which this benchmark is a meaningful readiness indicator.

level_value.description: A threshold value, optionally broken down by sex.

oneOf.description: Single value applying to all athletes.
Descriptive standard when a number doesn't apply. e.g. 'Unassisted pull-up'
